content: In this case what asset has the best safe risk reward for this scenario?

args.thought: Define the scenario parameters: US ground troops in Iran (limited ops vs. full invasion), probability-weighted outcomes, and what "safe risk/reward" means in this context. Safe = limited downside if wrong, meaningful upside if right.

Identify asset classes that historically benefit from geopolitical escalation: gold, oil, defense stocks, volatility products, safe-haven currencies (USD, CHF, JPY). Need to assess which has best risk/reward - limited downside if conflict doesn't escalate, meaningful upside if it does.

Assess risk/reward profile for each candidate asset. Gold: historically +10-25% during major geopolitical crises, but already near ATH (~$2,400-2,500/oz in early 2026 per memory). Downside if conflict de-escalates: -10-15%. Oil: more volatile, +20-40% on supply disruption fears, but demand destruction risk. Defense stocks: direct beneficiary but already priced in, single-stock risk. VIX products: severe decay, only for short-term trades. USD: safe haven but limited upside if Fed cutting. Need current price levels to assess starting valuations.

results.title: LMT - Lockheed Martin Financials - Stock Titan
results.content: Lockheed Martin has an [operating margin](https://www.stocktitan.net/articles/glossary/#operating-margin) of 10.3%, meaning the company retains $10 of operating profit per $100 of revenue. Lockheed Martin has a [free cash flow](https://www.stocktitan.net/articles/glossary/#free-cash-flow) margin of 9.2%, earning a moderate score of 46/100. Lockheed Martin earns a strong 74.7% [return on equity (ROE)](https://www.stocktitan.net/articles/glossary/#return-on-equity), meaning it generates $75 of profit for every $100 of shareholders' equity. For every $1 of reported earnings, Lockheed Martin generates $1.71 in operating cash flow ($8.6B OCF vs $5.0B net income). Yes, Lockheed Martin (LMT) reported a net income of $5.0B in fiscal year 2025, with a net profit margin of 6.7%. Lockheed Martin (LMT) had a net profit margin of 6.7% in fiscal year 2025, representing the share of revenue converted into profit after all expenses. Lockheed Martin (LMT) generated $6.9B in free cash flow during fiscal year 2025. Lockheed Martin (LMT) generated $8.6B in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2025, representing cash generated from core business activities.

results.title: Lockheed Martin Corp. (NYSE:LMT) | DuPont Analysis
results.content: | ROE | = | Net Profit Margin | × | Asset Turnover | × | Financial Leverage |. While ROE itself exhibits volatility, a closer examination of Net Profit Margin, Asset Turnover, and Financial Leverage reveals the underlying drivers of these changes. The substantial increase in ROE from 61.86% in 2022 to 101.24% in 2023 is primarily attributable to the combined effect of improved Net Profit Margin and significantly increased Financial Leverage. However, the subsequent decline in ROE to 84.26% in 2024 and 74.65% in 2025 is linked to a decrease in Net Profit Margin, despite continued high levels of Financial Leverage. :   The three-component DuPont analysis reveals that ROE is increasingly sensitive to changes in Net Profit Margin and Financial Leverage. However, ROA then decreased to 9.59% in 2024 and further to 8.38% in 2025, primarily due to the significant decline in Net Profit Margin, despite the relatively stable Asset Turnover. While Asset Turnover remained relatively stable, the fluctuations in Net Profit Margin had a more substantial impact on overall ROA performance.

results.title: Lockheed Martin Corp. (NYSE:LMT) | Analysis of Profitability Ratios
results.content: The decrease in margin in 2024, despite increased sales, suggests potential pressures on the cost of goods sold.Gross Profit Fluctuation Gross profit decreased from US$9,061 million in 2021 to US$8,287 million in 2022. Net profit margin = 100 × Net earnings ÷ Sales. Overall Trend The net profit margin began at 9.42% in 2021, decreased to 8.69% in 2022, increased significantly to 10.24% in 2023, and then declined sharply to 7.51% in 2024. Despite this sales growth, the net profit margin decreased overall, indicating that the increases in sales did not translate proportionally into higher profits. The largest sales increase occurred between 2024 and 2025, but this was accompanied by the largest decrease in net profit margin.Net Earnings Impact Net earnings peaked at US$6,920 million in 2023, coinciding with the highest net profit margin. While net earnings remained relatively stable between 2024 and 2025, the net profit margin declined, suggesting that cost of goods sold and/or operating expenses increased at a faster rate than sales during those years.

## About Crude Oil Trade NYMEX WTI Crude Oil futures (CL), the world’s most liquid crude oil contract. When traders need the current oil price, they check the WTI Crude Oil price. WTI (West Texas Intermediate, a US light sweet crude oil blend) futures provide direct crude oil exposure and are the most efficient way to trade oil after a sharp rise in US crude oil production. Use WTI Crude Oil futures to hedge against adverse oil price moves or speculate on whether WTI oil prices will rise or fall. Our diverse WTI futures and options suite provides more flexibility to trade oil with WTI Crude Oil price discovery. View delayed WTI Oil prices and WTI Oil price charts below. ### Looking for more?
